text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy. The surgeon makes three short incisions in the right thigh to harvest the gracilis muscle, including the tendon, up to the point where the anterior branch of the obturator nerve enters the muscle. The dissection is near the vascular pedicle, with the terminal branch of the circumflex femoral artery visible approximately ten centimeters below the pubic tubercle.

The surgeon spreads the harvested gracilis muscle forward to the perineal area, making an oval incision and wrapping the muscle around the anus. An additional small incision is made below the tip of the coccyx, and the tendon of the gracilis muscle is fixed with interrupted sutures to the periosteum of the lower surface of the axial tuberosity on the same side.
